mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals contracting residential investment and household balance-sheet stress that propagates to broader consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations for cyclical sectors deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1032K units, down sharply from 2006 peak above 2000K
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST 3-month trend and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or SPX posts positive returns in >55% of months with declining HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above a 5.0% threshold after a period of stability, it signals an inflection into labor-market deterioration consistent with early recession dynamics, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as forward earnings estimates are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 5.0% and continues rising for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5%, up from 4.4% low in 2007
regime_hint: labor market inflection from expansion to contraction
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE crosses above 5.0% and rises for 2+ months, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is held low while CPIAUCSL continues rising month-over-month, the Fed is constrained from easing further to support growth, creating a stagflationary regime in which equity risk premia rise and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days turn negative because monetary policy cannot offset a demand slowdown.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 2.5% while CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 4%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.98% while CPI level 215.132 implies elevated YoY inflation near 4-5%
failure_condition: During joint low-FEDFUNDS/high-CPI-growth periods, SPX 20d forward returns are not significantly negative relative to unconditional distribution (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) occurring late in an economic expansion signals capacity utilization rollover and impending margin compression for industrial firms, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter, but only when it coincides with an already-aged expansion rather than early-cycle noise.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.18, showing signs of plateauing after multi-year expansion
failure_condition: In late-cycle periods with 2+ months of INDPRO decline, SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances, invalidating the late-cycle conditioning.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer spending, the largest component of GDP, and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week as near-term earnings signals remain supportive.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 385448 million, near-term level data
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS MoM growth and forward 5-day SPX returns stays below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or positive RSAFS growth months show no return premium versus negative growth months (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A slowing trend in nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS), even before turning negative, signals weakening labor demand that leads equity markets to price in reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137754K, declining from prior peak in early 2008
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month PAYEMS growth trend and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX shows no significant negative drift following payroll deceleration (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS stabilizes after a rapid cutting cycle (rate-of-change flattens near zero for 2+ months), it signals the Fed believes policy is sufficiently accommodative, triggering a short-term relief rally in equities over the following trading week as easing-cycle uncertainty diminishes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS month-over-month change within +/-0.1pp for 2 consecutive months after prior cuts exceeding 0.5pp
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.98%, stabilized after aggressive cuts from 5.25% in 2007
regime_hint: end of monetary easing cycle, policy pause
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS stabilization show no significant positive premium versus unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a broad-based, cross-sector economic contraction rather than a sector-specific shock, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter because the breadth of weakness raises the probability of an economy-wide recession being priced into equities.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month AND INDPRO declines month-over-month simultaneously for 2+ months
evidence_refs: fact:both PAYEMS and INDPRO showing signs of concurrent weakening into mid-2008
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and INDPRO decline concurrently for 2+ months, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the joint signal's Rank IC is not superior to either single-series signal.
